preamble: A well-documented sound change where an open front vowel raises and becomes a diphthong, typically via insertion of a palatal offglide. The most famous example is the English Great Vowel Shift, where Middle English /aː/ evolved into Modern English /eɪ/.
sources: Labov, W. (1994). Principles of Linguistic Change, Volume I: Internal Factors. Blackwell.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics. Walter de Gruyter.
Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction. MIT Press.
